name: Branch Ergonomic Chair
introText: The Branch Ergonomic Chair delivers adjustable lumbar support, mesh back, and 3D armrests at $499 CAD — the best ergonomic chair under $600 for home office users who can't justify Herman Miller pricing.
whatWorks: Adjustable lumbar height and depth — the Branch allows you to position the lumbar pad exactly where your lower back needs it, which many chairs at this price don't offer.
Mesh back provides breathability without the premium price — functional ergonomics at $499 in a market where good ergonomic chairs typically start at $1,000.
worthKnowing: 5-year warranty vs. 12-year Herman Miller — reflects the quality difference. The Branch is built for home use, not commercial daily use at 8+ hours.
Branch ships directly from their website and through select Canadian retailers — check availability before expecting immediate delivery.

commonComplaints.title: Seat cushion compresses over time
commonComplaints.body: The foam seat cushion flattens after 12–18 months of daily use. Expected at this price, but a notable difference from Herman Miller's longer-lasting materials.

commonComplaints.title: Armrests less solid than premium chairs
commonComplaints.body: The 3D armrests flex slightly when leaning on them — functional but noticeably less rigid than Aeron or Leap V2 armrests.

commonComplaints.title: 5-year warranty reveals build quality gap
commonComplaints.body: The 5-year warranty vs. Herman Miller's 12 years reflects the actual expected lifespan difference. Plan for replacement in 5–7 years.

verdict: The Branch Ergonomic Chair is the best ergonomic chair under $600 — adjustable lumbar, mesh breathability, and solid build quality at a fraction of Herman Miller pricing. It won't last 12 years or match the PostureFit SL, but for a home office user who sits 6 hours or less daily, it's the smart value choice.

realBuyers.quote: Was going to buy the Aeron. Tried the Branch for a month. Good enough that I haven't looked back.
Back pain reduced significantly. For $499 I'm shocked at how well it works.
Students and home office workers — this is the one. No student needs to spend $1,800 on a chair.

metaDescription: Branch Ergonomic Chair review Canada: mesh back, adjustable lumbar, 3D armrests. $499 CAD. Best value ergonomic office chair under $600 for Canadians.
